title: 3 Legal Considerations for FX Notes
body_text:
3	Legal Considerations for FX Notes
3.1	Overview of Legal Considerations
Financial institutions are responsible for complying with applicable legislation and regulations. The information provided in this chapter is for convenience only and does not take the place of statutes and regulations. Refer to the actual statute or regulation for the full text. Note that there is no guarantee of accuracy for the online statutes.
Note:	Other federal and provincial legislation may influence foreign currency transactions (e.g., privacy legislation; human rights legislation). To address this issue, your financial institution must apply additional policies and procedures.
3.2	Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act and Regulations (Canada)
The
PCMLTFA
and
PCMLTF Regulations
apply to financial institutions and their agents (e.g., deposit-taking agents). Contact your financial institutionâs AML Compliance Officer or FINTRAC for more information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part.
3.3	Economic Sanctions
Certain Canadian regulations (e.g., the
Special Economic Measures (Zimbabwe) Regulations
, the
Regulations Implementing the United Nations Resolutions on the Democratic Peopleâs Republic of Korea
, the
Special Economic Measures (Burma) Regulations
) impose sanctions against designated countries and persons.
The sanctions vary but can include a prohibition(s) on:
Entering into or facilitating, directly or indirectly, any financial transaction to any person in a designated country
Providing or facilitating, directly or indirectly, any financial or other related service or transactions to a designated person
Dealing, directly or indirectly, in any property of a designated person, including funds derived or generated from property that is owned or controlled, directly or indirectly, by that person
Making any property available, directly or indirectly, for the benefit of a designated person
Providing financial or other related services or transactions for certain purposes (e.g., for the sale and supply of weapons)
These sanctions can also impose searching and reporting obligations on financial entities with respect to designated persons. These sanctions are subject to change.
Failure to comply with the regulations can result, upon conviction, in a fine and/or a prison term.
Note:	Third party determinations are important in determining if transactions are being undertaken indirectly for the benefit of designated persons.
See the Global Affairs Canada website for current information.
3.4	Agreements for FX Notes
To participate in FX Notes, your financial institution must enter into an agreement with Globex 2000. Contact your Account Executive for more information. See Section 1.3, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources.
END
